changes: OpenClaw still did not cut a new stable release, but its official showcase page remains the strongest fresh signal: the visible use-case surface expanded materially across home automation, visual briefings, accounting intake, job search, and other real-world workflows.
Poco shipped v0.3.2 on 2026-03-19, continuing the productized sandboxed-UX lane with another fast follow-up release.
ZeroClaw continued moving immediately after yesterday's stable v0.5.0 with fresh beta cuts, reinforcing that its Rust line is iterating at high speed rather than pausing after a milestone.
SwarmClaw extended its rapid release cadence again with v1.2.3 and v1.2.4 on 2026-03-18, keeping the orchestration/control-plane lane visibly active.

PicoClaw v0.2.3, IronClaw v0.19.0, Hermes Agent v2026.3.17, HiClaw v1.0.7, AstrBot v4.20.1, and TinyAGI v0.0.15 are all still very recent, so the broader field remains in an unusually high-churn release window.
whyItMatters: The clearest category signal is still breadth of deployed workflows, not just repo activity: OpenClaw's official showcase now gives stronger proof that messaging-native assistants are escaping demo-land and touching practical daily operations.
Poco, PicoClaw, ZeroClaw, and IronClaw together keep validating the main execution branches around lighter runtimes, safer runtimes, and more productized self-hosting experiences.

SwarmClaw, TinyAGI, and HiClaw keep the orchestration layer active, which suggests the market is starting to normalize teams, control planes, and manager-worker structures rather than treating them as side experiments.
The monitored set and top-level taxonomy still hold. This is ecosystem deepening and velocity, not a strategic map reset.
